source: DESNZ 'Next steps for electricity interconnection in Great Britain' (2026) for the current 10.3 GW fleet and the ElecLink/Viking Link/Greenlink figures; DESNZ/BEIS 'Electricity interconnectors in the UK since 2010' (Energy Trends special article, 30 June 2022), Table 1, for the established links.
basis_note: GB operational interconnector import capacity (not de-rated). Keys are the Elexon FUELHH INT* leg codes. The active capacity for any half-hour is the sum of the capacities of the legs that REPORT that half-hour: the settled FUELHH store blanks a leg before its link was commissioned, so this denominator tracks the fleet exactly as it grew (e.g. Greenlink appearing mid-2025) without needing exact commissioning dates. Net import as a share of this is the import capacity factor, the sibling of the wind/solar capacity factor (output / DUKES nameplate).
